name: Red Lobster Garlic Cheese Biscuits (Light)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment or waxed paper and spray with nonstick cooking spray.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Add 2 tablespoons of the margarine and blend with a pastry cutter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add buttermilk and cheese; combine with a fork, handling dough as little as possible. Drop spoonfuls onto the prepared baking sheet. Bake until lightly browned, about 8-10 minutes.
3. While the biscuits are baking, in a small microwavable bowl, add the remaining tablespoon of margarine. Microwave on high until the margarine is melted. Stir in garlic powder; set aside.
4. When the biscuits are done, remove from the oven and brush the margarine mixture evenly over the warm biscuits. Remove from the baking sheet and serve immediately.
Serving (1 biscuit) equals 3/4 fat, 1 1/4 bread, 15 calories. Per serving: 83 calories, 2 grams protein, 9 grams carbohydrate, 4 grams fat, 338 milligrams sodium, 4 milligrams cholesterol, 0 grams fiber.
